title: The early ferry I take to work
summary: Describe the small dawn ferry across the river and the quiet pilot who carries the same passengers to work each day.
topic: A small dawn ferry that carries workers across a river
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a small everyday journey you make and the person who runs it (a ferry, a bus, a shared van). Describe the journey, the people on board, and the quiet worker who does the same job every day. Use present simple, adverbs of frequency, and 'because' to explain why the job matters.
hintStarters: Every morning at six, I walk down to the river and…
The same people are always on the ferry, because…
The pilot never says much, but…
sampleText: Every morning at six, I walk down to the river and wait for the small ferry. The boat is old and grey, and it carries about twenty people across the water. The same people are always on the ferry, because we all work on the other side. A fruit seller, two nurses, and a young teacher stand near me every day. The pilot is an old man with strong hands, and his name is Mr Hong. He starts the engine slowly and ties the rope when we reach the other bank. He never says much, but he always nods at each passenger. The river is calm in the morning, and the water is grey and quiet. Mr Hong drives this ferry every day, even in the rain and the cold wind. His job is small, but it matters because none of us can cross the river without him. We pay a little money, and he keeps the old boat clean and safe. Sometimes I think the ferry is like a small floating village. When I step off, I always wave to Mr Hong, and he waves back.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chở khoảng hai mươi người qua sông

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The same people are always on the ferry

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một người bán trái cây, hai y tá và một cô giáo trẻ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third-person -s and adverbs of frequency
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Vì chủ ngữ là 'he', động từ thêm -s: 'he starts', 'he ties', 'he nods'. Trạng từ tần suất như 'always' và 'never' đứng trước động từ chính: 'he always nods', 'he never says much'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Because the subject is 'he', the verb takes -s: 'he starts', 'he ties', 'he nods'. Frequency adverbs like 'always' and 'never' go before the main verb: 'he always nods', 'he never says much'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'because' to give a reason
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' + một mệnh đề đầy đủ để nêu lý do: 'His job is small, but it matters because none of us can cross the river without him.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'because' + a full clause to give a reason: 'His job is small, but it matters because none of us can cross the river without him.'

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng lúc sáu giờ, tôi đi bộ xuống sông và chờ chuyến phà nhỏ. Chiếc thuyền cũ và xám, và nó chở khoảng hai mươi người qua sông. Vẫn những người ấy luôn ở trên phà, vì tất cả chúng tôi đều làm việc ở bờ bên kia. Một người bán trái cây, hai y tá và một cô giáo trẻ đứng cạnh tôi mỗi ngày. Người lái phà là một ông lão có đôi tay khỏe, và ông ấy tên là Mr Hong. Ông khởi động máy thật chậm và buộc dây thừng khi chúng tôi tới bờ bên kia. Ông không nói nhiều, nhưng ông luôn gật đầu với từng hành khách. Con sông yên ả vào buổi sáng, và mặt nước xám và tĩnh lặng. Mr Hong lái chiếc phà này mỗi ngày, cả trong mưa và gió lạnh. Công việc của ông nhỏ bé, nhưng nó quan trọng vì không ai trong chúng tôi qua sông được nếu thiếu ông. Chúng tôi trả một ít tiền, và ông giữ chiếc thuyền cũ sạch sẽ và an toàn. Đôi khi tôi nghĩ chiếc phà giống như một ngôi làng nhỏ nổi trên nước. Khi tôi bước xuống, tôi luôn vẫy tay chào Mr Hong, và ông vẫy lại.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes your morning routine?
options: Every morning at six, I walks down to the river and wait for the small ferry.
Every morning at six, I walk down to the river and wait for the small ferry.
Every morning at six, I walking down to the river and wait for the small ferry.
Every morning at six, down to the river I walk and the small ferry wait for.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the verbs are base form ('walk', 'wait'), and the word order is natural.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does the verb take with 'I'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the ferry most naturally?
options: The boat is old and grey, and it carry about twenty people across the water.
The boat are old and grey, and it carries about twenty people across the water.
The boat is old and grey, and it carries about twenty people across the water.
The boat is old and greyly, and about twenty people across the water it carries.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'boat is' is singular, and 'it carries' takes the third-person -s.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the form of 'be' and the verb ending with 'it'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why the same people are on the ferry?
options: The same people is always on the ferry, because we all work on the other side.
The same people are always on the ferry, because of we all work on the other side.
The same people always are on the ferry, because on the other side we all work it.
The same people are always on the ferry, because we all work on the other side.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'people are' is plural, 'always' follows 'are', and 'because' has a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Watch the plural 'people' and the place of 'always' with 'are'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence lists the passengers correctly?
options: A fruit seller, two nurses, and a young teacher stand near me every day.
A fruit seller, two nurses, and a young teacher stands near me every day.
A fruit seller, two nurse, and a young teacher stand near me every day.
A fruit seller, two nurses, and a young teacher near me stand every day them.
mcQuestions.explanation: The subject is plural, so 'stand' has no -s, and 'nurses' is the correct plural.
mcQuestions.hint: Is the whole subject singular or plural?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'He ___ the engine slowly.'
options: start
starts
starting
started

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses adverbs of frequency correctly with 'but'?
options: He never say much, but he always nod at each passenger.
He says never much, but he nods always at each passenger.
He never says much, but he always nods at each passenger.
He never says much, but at each passenger he always nods it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'says' and 'nods' agree with 'he'; 'never' and 'always' go before the main verbs.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb endings and the place of 'never'/'always'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the river in the morning?
options: The river are calm in the morning, and the water is grey and quiet.
The river is calmly in the morning, and the water is grey and quiet.
The river is calm in the morning, and grey and quiet the water is it.
The river is calm in the morning, and the water is grey and quiet.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'river is' and 'water is' are singular, and 'calm' is the correct adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Check 'be' agreement and use an adjective after 'is'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence says he works every day, even in bad weather?
options: Mr Hong drives this ferry every day, even in the rain and the cold wind.
Mr Hong drive this ferry every day, even in the rain and the cold wind.
Mr Hong drives this ferry every days, even in the rain and the cold wind.
Mr Hong drives this ferry every day, even in the rain and the cold wind he drives.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'drives' agrees with 'Mr Hong', and 'every day' is correct (singular 'day').
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb ending and the phrase 'every day'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why his job matters?
options: His job is small, but it matter because none of us can cross the river without him.
His job is small, but it matters because none of us can cross the river without him.
His job is small, but it matters because of none of us can cross the river without him.
His job is small, but it matters because the river without him none of us can cross it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'it matters' agrees with 'it', and 'because' is followed by a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb ending and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best closes the description?
options: When I step off, I always waves to Mr Hong, and he wave back.
When I step off, I wave always to Mr Hong, and he waves back.
When I step off, I always wave to Mr Hong, and he waves back.
When I step off, to Mr Hong I always wave, and back he waves it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I wave' (no -s) and 'he waves' (with -s) both agree, and 'always' is before the main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the verb endings to 'I' and 'he'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do we so often overlook the people who carry us through our daily routines, and who is one such worker you depend on without thinking about it?
